meaning: vi.狂热地描述,写狂想文,作狂想曲vt.狂热地说
examples: And it's easy for the experts to rhapsodize about gratitude. But there are merits to tempering your complaints.
Marketers rhapsodize about selling to 1.3 billion consumers, while agronomists worry about whether those 1.3 billion mouths will have enough to eat.
Most would rhapsodize about being able to immediately put that extra power to good use.
But now the long-snooty brand's camel-black-red-and-white check drapes rap singers who rhapsodize about it in their gritty songs.
While it is commonplace in baseball for fans and commentators to embellish or to rhapsodize poetically about the feats of ball players, Albert Pujols has become a mythical character.
